print.signature: func print(_ items: Any..., separator: String = " ", terminator: String = "\n")
print.description: Writes textual representations of the given items to standard output

debugPrint.signature: func debugPrint(_ items: Any..., separator: String = " ", terminator: String = "\n")
debugPrint.description: Writes debug textual representations of the given items to standard output

dump.signature: func dump<T>(_ value: T, name: String? = nil, indent: Int = 0, maxDepth: Int = .max, maxItems: Int = .max) -> T
dump.description: Dumps an object's contents using its mirror to standard output

min.signature: func min<T: Comparable>(_ x: T, _ y: T) -> T
min.description: Returns the lesser of two comparable values

max.signature: func max<T: Comparable>(_ x: T, _ y: T) -> T
max.description: Returns the greater of two comparable values

abs.signature: func abs<T: Comparable & SignedNumeric>(_ x: T) -> T
abs.description: Returns the absolute value of the given number

stride.signature: func stride<T>(from start: T, to end: T, by stride: T.Stride) -> StrideTo<T>
stride.description: Returns a sequence from start to (but not including) end, stepping by the specified amount

zip.signature: func zip<Sequence1, Sequence2>(_ sequence1: Sequence1, _ sequence2: Sequence2) -> Zip2Sequence<Sequence1, Sequence2>
zip.description: Creates a sequence of pairs built out of two underlying sequences

map.signature: func map<T>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> T) rethrows -> [T]
map.description: Returns an array containing the results of mapping the given closure over the sequence's elements

filter.signature: func filter(_ isIncluded: (Element) throws -> Bool) rethrows -> [Element]
filter.description: Returns an array containing only the elements that satisfy the given predicate

reduce.signature: func reduce<Result>(_ initialResult: Result, _ nextPartialResult: (Result, Element) throws -> Result) rethrows -> Result
reduce.description: Returns the result of combining the sequence's elements using the given closure

compactMap.signature: func compactMap<ElementOfResult>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> ElementOfResult?) rethrows -> [ElementOfResult]
compactMap.description: Returns an array containing the non-nil results of calling the given transformation

flatMap.signature: func flatMap<SegmentOfResult: Sequence>(_ transform: (Element) throws -> SegmentOfResult) rethrows -> [SegmentOfResult.Element]
flatMap.description: Returns an array containing the concatenated results of calling the given transformation

sorted.description: Returns the elements of the sequence, sorted

forEach.signature: func forEach(_ body: (Element) throws -> Void) rethrows
forEach.description: Calls the given closure on each element in the sequence in the same order as a for-in loop

contains.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the sequence contains the given element

first.description: The first element of the collection, or nil if the collection is empty

last.description: The last element of the collection, or nil if the collection is empty

count.description: The number of elements in the collection

isEmpty.description: A Boolean value indicating whether the collection is empty

append.description: Adds a new element at the end of the array

insert.signature: mutating func insert(_ newElement: Element, at i: Int)
insert.description: Inserts a new element at the specified position

remove.signature: mutating func remove(at index: Int) -> Element
remove.description: Removes and returns the element at the specified position

removeAll.signature: mutating func removeAll(keepingCapacity keepCapacity: Bool = false)
removeAll.description: Removes all elements from the array

enumerated.description: Returns a sequence of pairs (n, x), where n represents a consecutive integer starting at zero and x represents an element of the sequence

prefix.description: Returns a subsequence, up to the specified maximum length, containing the initial elements

suffix.description: Returns a subsequence, up to the given maximum length, containing the final elements

joined.description: Returns a new string by concatenating the elements of the sequence, adding the given separator between each element

split.signature: func split(separator: Character, maxSplits: Int = .max, omittingEmptySubsequences: Bool = true) -> [Substring]
split.description: Returns the longest possible subsequences of the collection, around elements equal to the given element

replacingOccurrences.signature: func replacingOccurrences(of target: String, with replacement: String) -> String
replacingOccurrences.description: Returns a new string in which all occurrences of a target string are replaced by another given string

hasPrefix.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the string begins with the specified prefix

hasSuffix.description: Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the string ends with the specified suffix

uppercased.description: Returns an uppercased version of the string

lowercased.description: Returns a lowercased version of the string

trimmingCharacters.description: Returns a new string made by removing from both ends of the string characters contained in a given character set

precondition.signature: func precondition(_ condition: @autoclosure () -> Bool, _ message: @autoclosure () -> String = "", file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line)
precondition.description: Checks a necessary condition for making forward progress

assert.signature: func assert(_ condition: @autoclosure () -> Bool, _ message: @autoclosure () -> String = "", file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line)
assert.description: Performs a traditional C-style assert with an optional message

fatalError.signature: func fatalError(_ message: @autoclosure () -> String = "", file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line) -> Never
fatalError.description: Unconditionally prints a given message and stops execution

type(of:).description: Returns the dynamic type of a value

withAnimation.signature: func withAnimation<Result>(_ animation: Animation? = .default, _ body: () throws -> Result) rethrows -> Result
withAnimation.description: Returns the result of recomputing the view's body with the provided animation

Bool.description: A value type whose instances are either true or false

Dictionary.signature: struct Dictionary<Key: Hashable, Value>: Collection, ExpressibleByDictionaryLiteral
Dictionary.description: A collection whose elements are key-value pairs

Set.description: An unordered collection of unique elements

Optional.description: A type that represents either a wrapped value or the absence of a value

Result.description: A value that represents either a success or a failure, including an associated value in each case

URL.description: A value that identifies the location of a resource, such as an item on a remote server or the path to a local file

Date.signature: struct Date: Comparable, Hashable, Codable, Sendable
Date.description: A specific point in time, independent of any calendar or time zone

Error.description: A type representing an error value that can be thrown

Codable.description: A type that can convert itself into and out of an external representation

Hashable.description: A type that can be hashed into a Hasher to produce an integer hash value

Equatable.description: A type that can be compared for value equality

Comparable.description: A type that can be compared using the relational operators <, <=, >=, and >

Identifiable.signature: protocol Identifiable { associatedtype ID: Hashable; var id: ID { get } }
Identifiable.description: A class of types whose instances hold the value of an entity with stable identity

Sendable.description: A type whose values can safely be passed across concurrency domains by copying

AsyncSequence.description: A type that provides asynchronous, sequential, iterated access to its elements

Void.description: The return type of functions that don't explicitly specify a return type

Never.description: The return type of functions that do not return normally, that is, a type with no values

Any.description: The protocol to which all types implicitly conform

AnyObject.description: The protocol to which all classes implicitly conform

Range.description: A half-open interval from a lower bound up to, but not including, an upper bound

ClosedRange.description: An interval from a lower bound up to, and including, an upper bound

Sequence.description: A type that provides sequential, iterated access to its elements

Collection.description: A sequence whose elements can be traversed multiple times, nondestructively, and accessed by an indexed subscript

JSONDecoder.description: An object that decodes instances of a data type from JSON objects

JSONEncoder.description: An object that encodes instances of a data type as JSON objects

URLSession.description: An object that coordinates a group of related network data transfer tasks

DispatchQueue.description: An object that manages the execution of work items serially or concurrently on your app's main thread or on a background thread

NotificationCenter.description: A notification dispatch mechanism that enables the broadcast of information to registered observers

UserDefaults.description: An interface to the user's defaults database, where you store key-value pairs persistently across launches
